next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 3

She found him by the smell.

Two days north of the settlement where she'd healed the farmer, late-spring air went sweet and rotten at once. Vel stepped off the road into a meadow churned flat by one man's panic. A Sustenance Charm had gone off there with banquet logic: loaves split open by fox teeth, fruit liquefying in the grass, a metal basin of stew sagging as the conjured shape gave up. Enough food remained to feed a caravan, if the caravan liked flies.

That alone told her most of what she needed. Kem Aldraste had left the Vault three days ago at three hundred. The gate story had outrun him. She put the Projection Shield at three, maybe four, and the feast at eight if he'd overshot badly, ten if he'd been spectacular about it. When she added the smaller comforts that always tempted people with high numbers, the total made her stop in the road.

He was at two hundred and eighty-six, give or take.

At twenty she had still been rich enough to miss a spend and keep moving. At thirty-one the Ledger never shut up, and fourteen levels in three days for food, warmth, and whatever else had offended him made her jaw lock.

Four hundred meters farther on, she found him sitting on a fallen log with his boots off and one foot in his lap, studying a blister with scholarly concern. His road boots were soft Vault leather, good for polished floors and not much else. One sole had split. As Vel watched, a pale Glow rose over the raw skin and cost him a level just to inspect his own foot.

She stayed in the tree line long enough to take him in. He was tall, mid-twenties, with...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Conservatory Rite — the Hoarded philosophy of inaction performed as ritual
Lord Aldraste's Stillness: the cost of never acting, rendered physically
Kem's first ability use — absurdly overpowered for opening a door
Kem's immediate incompetence outside the Vault: Sustenance Charm that feeds forty
Kem takes the House signet from the ceremonial stand by the gate — not because he wants it, but because leaving it would mean he'd thought this through
Kem passes the sealed wing the Hoarded never discuss — a different quality of silence
prev_tail_excerpt:
...roads. Fresh enough that the search around him would still be widening. Searchers were part of the cost now. So was the possibility that she reached him and found nothing useful at all, only a panicked aristocrat bleeding levels into ditches because he had finally learned his hands could do things.

He might refuse her. He might already be half caught. He might never have wanted zero in the first place.

Vel wrapped the journal back in oilcloth and slid it flat against the inside of the pack. Then she tightened the straps and set the pack by the cot. At first light she would go north, listen at every stop, and find the place where rumor turned back into tracks.

Dark filled the storeroom. The window became a blue square. Sorren's mule chewed in the yard with steady animal indifference. A door shut somewhere. Steps crossed the packed earth. The well pulley creaked once, then stopped.

Thirty-one sat behind her sternum, exact as ever. The journal rested within arm's reach. Beyond it, south, lay the route she had been studying for eight years. Beyond that, if she was right, the door. The flash of it and the absence it had left came back for a second, and she shut that thought down before it could widen.

Vel lay down on the cot without undressing. The last calculation before sleep was a subtraction problem with bad numbers in it: thirty-one minus the road north, minus whatever it cost to catch up to House Aldraste's son, minus whatever it took to make him understand what she was asking. She kept one hand looped through the pack strap until sometime in the dark her grip let go.
secondary_character_beats: Lady Aldraste says Kem's name once as he leaves — maternal feeling compressed into a single word

setups_to_plant.description: Lord Aldraste's deterioration from level-300 preservation — the Hoarded philosophy has a physical cost they refuse to name
Brief reference in Kem's awareness of the Vault — a wing or quarter the Hoarded never discuss, where certain people are permitted to spend
Kem takes his House signet — a physical marker of Hoarded identity carried impulsively rather than deliberately, tracking his changing relationship to his origin

state_deltas_required: Kem's revulsion at the Hoarded lifestyle and his father's visible deterioration into Stillness
Kem breaks from the Hoarded; free, incompetent, and dropping fast
